body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization` **Service directory:** `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/` **Triggered by:** commit 9cf12364688dd210b5c907b1a0199dd2e46b87eb (PR #58429) by @azure-sdk ## What Changed The package version was incremented after the `1.0.0` stable release (with a patch release `1.0.1` on 2026-04-20 upgrading `Azure.Core` and `Azure.ResourceManager` dependencies). The package is now fully GA with no service-specific documentation examples. ## Gaps Found - **Examples section is generic** — the README only links to the generic .NET Management Library samples page (`https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples`) with no service-specific code snippets demonstrating how to query carbon emissions data, list emissions by subscription, or export emissions reports - **Key concepts section is a stub** — only links to the general Azure SDK introduction; no carbon-optimization-specific concepts explained (e.g., emissions data models, query scopes, date ranges, carbon emissions breakdown by resource/subscription) - **No test-backed code snippets** — the `tests/` directory has no `Samples/` subfolder; there are no `#region Snippet:` markers to drive README code injection via `Update-Snippets.ps1` - **Non-standard "Documentation" section present** — the README contains a standalone `## Documentation` section (with Quickstart, API References, and Authentication links) that is not part of the canonical README template; these links should be folded into the appropriate standard sections - **Install section missing dotnet CLI version qualifier** — no explicit stable version pinned in the install snippet, which is helpful at GA
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify the README **File:** `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/README.md` **Action:** Edit 1. Remove the standalone `## Documentation` section; move the Quickstart and Authentication links into **Getting started → Authenticate the Client** and **Next steps** respectively. 2. Expand the `## Key concepts` section to describe: - `CarbonServiceClient` — the entry point for querying carbon emissions - Query scope (subscription, resource group, resource) - Date range parameters for emissions queries - Emissions data model overview 3. Replace the `## Examples` section body with snippet placeholders (see Step 2 below). Canonical reference for structure and tone: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` --- ### Step 2: Add test-backed code snippets Create the file `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/tests/Samples/Sample1_QueryCarbonEmissions.cs` with the following content: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization; using Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization.Models; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_QueryCarbonEmissions : SamplesBase { [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task QueryCarbonEmissionsForSubscription() { #region Snippet:QueryCarbonEmissions // Create an ArmClient with DefaultAzureCredential ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); // Get the Carbon emissions client from the tenant resource TenantResource tenant = client.GetTenants().GetAllAsync().ToBlockingEnumerable().First(); CarbonServiceClient carbonClient = tenant.GetCarbonServiceClient(); // Query total carbon emissions for a subscription over a date range string subscriptionId = ""; var queryBody = new CarbonEmissionQueryRequestBody( reportType: CarbonEmissionQueryReportType.OverallSummaryReport, dateRange: new CarbonEmissionQueryDateRange( start: new DateTimeOffset(2024, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, TimeSpan.Zero), end: new DateTimeOffset(2024, 12, 31, 0, 0, 0, TimeSpan.Zero) ) ); queryBody.SubscriptionList.Add(subscriptionId); CarbonEmissionDataListResult result = await carbonClient.QueryCarbonEmissionReportsAsync(queryBody); foreach (CarbonEmissionData item in result.Value) { Console.WriteLine($"Resource: {item.ResourceId}, Carbon (kgCO2e): {item.TotalCarbonEmission}"); } #endregion } } } ``` Add a snippet placeholder in the `## Examples` section of the README at the location shown below: ```markdown ## Examples ### Query carbon emissions for a subscription The following example shows how to query carbon emissions for a subscription over a calendar year: ```C# Snippet:QueryCarbonEmissions ``` ``` > **Note:** The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will replace the content between the snippet fences with the actual code from the test file. Adjust the exact method signatures and model types to match what is available in the generated `src/Generated/` folder. --- ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/README.md` must contain these sections in this order: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present (link only — expand) | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Present but stub — expand with service-specific content | | Examples | ⚠️ Present but generic link only — replace with snippets | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ✅ Present | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Sections to fix: - **Key concepts**: Replace generic SDK link with a 3–5 bullet description of the CarbonOptimization data model and client entry points - **Examples**: Replace the generic `aka.ms` link with inline snippet placeholders - **Remove**: The non-standard `## Documentation` section (move its links into Getting started and Next steps) --- ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 carbon` — confirms README was updated with snippet code 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 carbon` — regenerates API listing (run only if public API changed) 5. `dotnet format sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above.
title: [Carbon] Docs: Add service-specific examples and key concepts to Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization README
